title: Momentum Ecosystem Manifest
description: Declares a set of related momentum-installed repos that coordinate as one product. Lives at the root of an ecosystem repo (sibling to its member repos).

name.description: Lowercase slug identifying the ecosystem. Used as the default folder name for `momentum ecosystem init`.

version.description: Manifest schema version. Locked to 1 until a breaking change.

created.description: ISO-8601 date the ecosystem manifest was created.

members.description: Repos belonging to this ecosystem. Each entry references a sibling directory on disk.

items.description: A member needs at least one of `path` (co-located on disk) or `remote` (a git URL). Both may be present: `path` is the local checkout, `remote` lets teammates on other machines resolve the same member (ADR-0015).

id.description: Stable slug for this member. Used in dependency edges and session log entries.

path.description: Filesystem path to the member repo, relative to the ecosystem root. Optional when `remote` is set (a teammate may not have this member checked out locally).

remote.description: Git URL of the member repo (ADR-0015). Lets a distributed team share one ecosystem without identical folder layouts; `momentum ecosystem status` resolves the member by URL when no local `path` exists.

role.description: Coarse role classification. Used by /track and consumers to render groupings.

owns.description: Optional list of named artifacts this member owns (e.g. `openapi.json`, `aws.account.XXX`). Free-form identifiers.

consumes.description: Optional list of named artifacts this member consumes from siblings.

dependencies.description: Directed edges between members. Used for cross-repo coordination (deploy order, contract drift).
